content: Dashboard view-model types live at src/dashboard/types.ts, intentionally separate from src/core/types.ts. The split: core/types.ts = durable domain projection of DB rows (Burrow/Run/RunEvent/Message); dashboard/types.ts = presentation projection for burrow watch and future burrow serve, with derived fields (activeRun), trimmed payloads (RunSummary omits prompt/metadata), and capped tails (eventTail, default 500). Keeping the projection in its own module avoids leaking presentation concerns into core types.

DashboardSnapshot follows the same additive-only versioning discipline as the §14.1 event envelope. Rules: (1) existing keys never change semantics or types; (2) new top-level + per-interface keys may be added, consumers MUST ignore unknown fields; (3) DASHBOARD_SNAPSHOT_VERSION (currently 1) bumps ONLY on breaking change — adding fields or enum members does not bump it; (4) optional fields stay optional forever; (5) enum members may be added, consumers MUST treat unknown state/kind/stream values as pass-through strings. The lock test (src/dashboard/types.test.ts) pins canonical key sets per interface so any rename/removal trips the test, forcing an intentional version bump.

name: wire-shape-key-set-lock-test
description: Lock test for stable wire shapes pins canonical key set per interface

content: Dashboard view-model types (BurrowCard.createdAt/updatedAt/destroyedAt, RunSummary.queuedAt/startedAt/completedAt, EventTailEntry.ts, DashboardSnapshot.ts) store timestamps as ISO-8601 strings, not Date objects. Reason: the envelope must round-trip through JSON without conversion (JSON.stringify(Date) emits a string but JSON.parse leaves it a string — using Date in the type would make the round-trip lossy). Mirrors the §14.1 event envelope (eventToEnvelope() converts Date to ISO string at the boundary). The conversion from row Date columns to ISO strings happens in the buildSnapshot builder (step 2).

name: id-based-tui-selection
description: ViewState (src/dashboard/tui-state.ts) tracks selectedBurrowId as an id (not index) so a snapshot that reorders burrows[] between ticks doesn't silently jump the cursor. Movement clamps via findIndex against the *current* snapshot; missing-id falls back to index 0. syncToSnapshot is a separate function (called per snapshot tick) that re-pins to the first remaining burrow when the selection disappears, drops mode back to list, and clamps detailScrollOffset to the new eventTail.length.

content: TUI reducer (src/dashboard/tui-state.ts reduce()) treats DashboardSnapshot as a *context* parameter, not state — pure (state, key, snapshot) -> state, never holds a reference past the call. No-op transitions (q after quit, j/k in detail mode, enter with null selection, esc in list mode, scroll in list mode) return the input state referentially-equal so downstream memoization is cheap. KeyName is a closed union; the switch is exhaustive (extending the union surfaces a missing-case error).

Detail-pane scroll offset (ViewState.detailScrollOffset) is 'lines back from the newest event' — offset 0 = live tail at bottom; pageUp INCREASES offset, pageDown DECREASES. Reducer clamps to [0, eventTail.length] using only the snapshot (termSize-unaware on purpose); the renderer applies a tighter viewport clamp once it knows actual terminal height. DETAIL_SCROLL_PAGE_SIZE is a fixed constant (10), not a function of termSize.
